L.description: The amount of time needed to cool in order to create a single tempered glass panel.

S.description: The amount of time needed for a regular glass pane to cool down.

P.description: Time needed to heat up to create a single tempered glass panel.

H.description: Earnings per each treated glass panel

T.description: Earnings from each standard-sized glass sheet

C.description: The duration of heating needed to make a single standard glass panel

D.description: The highest amount of time the heating machine can be used in a day.

V.description: The maximum time allotted for the cooling machine to operate each day.

assumptions.description: The amount of time needed to cool in order to create a single tempered glass panel must be non-negative.
The amount of time needed for a regular glass pane to cool down must be non-negative.
The time needed to heat up to create a single tempered glass panel must be non-negative.
The earnings per each treated glass panel must be non-negative.
The earnings from each standard-sized glass sheet must be non-negative.
The duration of heating needed to make a single standard glass panel must be non-negative.
The highest amount of time the heating machine can be used in a day must be non-negative.
The maximum time allotted for the cooling machine to operate each day must be non-negative.

h.description: The quantity of tempered glass sheets needed for production

e.description: The quantity of standard glass sheets needed for production.

constraints.description: The combined heating time needed to create Regular and Tempered glass panes does not go beyond D.

code.gurobipy: model.addConstr(C * e + P * h <= D, name="HeatingTime")

constraints.description: The combined time needed to cool both Regular and Tempered glass panels does not go beyond V.
The number of regular panes produced is non-negative.
The number of tempered panes produced is non-negative.

objective.description: The aim is to increase the overall profit by maximizing the total value obtained from T multiplied by the quantity of Regular panes and H multiplied by the quantity of Tempered panes.
